tool.name: generate_smart_payloads
tool.description:
P6 ELITE: Generate intelligent context-aware payloads with learning.
This is the most advanced payload generation function that: - Learns from previous successful/failed payloads (hypothesis ledger) - Adapts to target technology stack (framework, database, OS, WAF) - Avoids blocked patterns and optimizes payload selection - Generates framework-specific payload variations - Creates WAF bypass mutations automatically
USE THIS FIRST when you have target context information (framework, WAF, etc). Falls back to standard generators if no context available.
Integration with Hypothesis Ledger: - Loads failed payloads to avoid similar ones - Prioritizes payloads similar to successful ones - Tracks blocked WAF patterns to evade them

parameter.name: framework
parameter.description:
Detected framework: "django", "laravel", "spring", "flask", "rails", "express"
Framework-specific optimizations: - django/flask: Prioritizes Jinja2 template injection patterns - laravel: Blade template patterns - spring: Thymeleaf and Freemarker patterns - express: EJS and Pug patterns

parameter.name: database
parameter.description:
Database type: "mysql", "postgresql", "mssql", "oracle"
Optimizes SQLi payloads for database-specific syntax

parameter.name: os
parameter.description:
Operating system: "linux", "windows"
Optimizes command injection for target OS

parameter.name: waf_type
parameter.description:
WAF detected: "cloudflare", "akamai", "modsecurity", "imperva", "f5"
Automatically generates bypass variations for detected WAF

parameter.name: hypothesis_id
parameter.description:
Hypothesis ID from ledger to learn from previous attempts
When provided, loads: - Previously tested payloads (to avoid redundancy) - Successful payloads (to generate similar variants) - Blocked patterns (to evade WAF signatures)

returns.description: Response containing: - 'payloads': Intelligently selected payloads with priority scoring - 'total_count': Number of payloads returned - 'context': Full PayloadContext object with detection info - 'intelligence': Metadata about optimizations applied: - framework_optimized: Whether framework-specific payloads prioritized - waf_aware: Whether WAF bypass variations included - learning_enabled: Whether hypothesis ledger integration active - tech_stack: Summary of detected technologies - 'usage': Instructions for testing

tool.workflow: 1. Detect target technology stack (framework, database, OS, WAF) 2. Call generate_smart_payloads() with detected context 3. If testing an existing hypothesis, include hypothesis_id 4. Tool builds PayloadContext and applies intelligent filtering: - Excludes payloads similar to failed attempts - Prioritizes payloads similar to successful ones - Optimizes for detected framework/database - Generates WAF bypass variations 5. Test returned payloads sequentially 6. Record results in hypothesis ledger 7. On subsequent iterations, payloads adapt based on learning

tool.name: generate_xss_payloads
tool.description:
Generate context-aware XSS (Cross-Site Scripting) payloads.
Creates payloads optimized for specific injection contexts with optional WAF bypass techniques and encoding options.
Use this when you've identified a potential XSS injection point.

parameter.name: context
parameter.description: Injection context: - "html" (default) - Standard HTML body injection - "attribute" - Inside HTML attribute values - "javascript" - Inside JavaScript code - "href" - Inside href/src attributes - "template" - Inside template expressions - "multi" - All contexts

returns.description: Response containing: - 'payloads': List of XSS payloads with metadata - 'total_count': Number of payloads returned - 'context': Injection context - 'usage': Instructions for testing

tool.name: generate_sqli_payloads
tool.description:
Generate context-aware SQL injection payloads.
Creates payloads for different database backends and injection types including UNION, time-based blind, error-based, and stacked queries.
Use after detecting potential SQLi injection points.

parameter.name: column_count
parameter.description: Number of columns for UNION payloads (if known)

returns.description: Response containing: - 'payloads': List of SQLi payloads with metadata - 'total_count': Number of payloads - 'db_type': Target database - 'usage': Testing instructions

tool.name: generate_xxe_payloads
tool.description:
Generate XXE (XML External Entity) injection payloads.
Creates payloads for file disclosure, SSRF via XXE, and blind XXE data exfiltration attacks.
Use when XML processing is detected (SOAP, XML APIs, file uploads).

parameter.name: callback_url
parameter.description: Attacker callback URL for blind XXE (e.g., "http://attacker.com")

returns.description: Response containing: - 'payloads': List of XXE payloads with metadata - 'total_count': Number of payloads - 'usage': Testing instructions

tool.name: generate_ssti_payloads
tool.description:
Generate SSTI (Server-Side Template Injection) payloads.
Creates payloads for various template engines: Jinja2, Twig, Freemarker, Velocity, ERB, Smarty, Thymeleaf.
Use when template engine rendering is detected.

returns.description: Response containing: - 'payloads': List of SSTI payloads with metadata - 'detection_tips': Tips for identifying SSTI

tool.name: generate_cmd_injection_payloads
tool.description:
Generate OS command injection payloads.
Creates payloads for command chaining, time-based blind detection, and out-of-band exfiltration.
Use when application executes system commands with user input.

returns.description: Response containing: - 'payloads': List of command injection payloads - 'tips': Testing tips
